title: Syntax error for attribute in front of statement
body:
While looking at #2021 I noticed that the verilog standard allows attributes in more places than yosys currently accepts, such as e.g. in front of any statement (including the null statement).
```
module test ();
	localparam y = 1;
	always @(*)
		if (y) (* foo *) ;
endmodule
```
```
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: stmt.v
Parsing Verilog input from `stmt.v' to AST representation.
stmt.v:4: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected ';'
```
As attributes have no defined meaning, we should accept and ignore any unknown attribute in any of the allowed locations.
code_blocks:
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: stmt.v
Parsing Verilog input from `stmt.v' to AST representation.
stmt.v:4: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected ';'
